Looking at the code, I need to identify the bug in the `upload_gate` function.

The function is supposed to return `(allowed, remaining)` where `allowed` is `True` if the caller may proceed, and `remaining` is the number of uploads still available (0 when gate is closed).

The key issue is on line 74: `if len(active) > limit:` should be `if len(active) >= limit:`.

When `len(active) == limit`, the window is already at capacity, so the upload should NOT be allowed (gate should be closed, returning `False, 0`). But with `>`, when `len(active) == limit`, the condition is `False`, so it falls through to line 76 returning `True, limit - len(active)` which would be `True, 0`. This is contradictory - it says allowed but remaining is 0.

Also on line 76, when `len(active) == limit`, `remaining` would be `0`, but `allowed` is `True`. The spec says remaining is 0 when the gate is closed, implying if remaining is 0, the gate should be closed. So the boundary condition is wrong.

The fix should be `>=` on line 74 instead of `>`.
